level_signals: Founding PM for a major new product pillar
Works directly with founders and executive team
Comp range $240K–$320K base, consistent with Staff/Principal IC at a growth-stage startup
JD states 'we focus on impact and potential for growth more than years of experience'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Zero-to-one track record. Products you built from nothing to meaningful revenue, ideally at a B2B SaaS company. Domain matters less than evidence you can find product-market fit under uncertainty.

Technical depth. Can evaluate architectural tradeoffs, debate infrastructure decisions with senior engineers, and reason about things like batch vs. real-time and caching strategies at the level of product decisions.
Commercial instinct. Think in terms of pipeline, conversion, and expansion. Build roadmaps that map to revenue targets.
Speed. Small team, high autonomy, tight cycles. We use AI heavily in our own workflows.
Cross-functional trust. This spans engineering, design, sales, CS, and leadership. You drive clarity from ambiguity.

Customer discovery. Deep engagement with product and growth teams at enterprise companies to understand how they personalize web and mobile today, and what would earn their trust in an agent-driven approach.
Scale the GTM. Co-own pipeline, join customer calls, shape competitive positioning, partner with CS on implementation playbooks.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Products you built from nothing to meaningful revenue, ideally at a B2B SaaS company. Domain matters less than evidence you can find product-market fit under uncertainty.

scope.surface: Agentic Personalization — Hightouch's third major product pillar covering real-time web and mobile personalization via browser SDKs and edge delivery
primary_metrics_inferred: Pipeline and revenue tied to Agentic Personalization pillar
Experiment volume run by customer teams
Conversion, retention, and revenue lift for customers
Product-market fit milestones / revenue milestones on roadmap
core_responsibilities: Define the product, sequencing, and go-to-market for Agentic Personalization
Deep engagement with enterprise product and growth teams for customer discovery
Define the agent experience — what agents do autonomously vs. what needs human approval
Design the feedback loop that builds trust over time
Co-own pipeline, join customer calls, shape competitive positioning
Partner with CS on implementation playbooks
Translate customer discovery into a roadmap tied to revenue milestones

compensation.equity_form: ISO options with early exercise and 10-year post-termination exercise window

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($240K–$320K base) and labeled location-independent
Equity is ISOs with early exercise and generous 10-year post-termination window — founder-friendly terms
High-visibility founding role with direct access to founders and executive team
Clear product narrative and market context provided — not a vague or poorly-defined role
JD states 'we focus on impact and potential for growth more than years of experience' — open to non-traditional backgrounds
1,000+ enterprise customers provides strong sales motion and customer discovery opportunity
red_flags: E-Verify participation combined with no explicit visa sponsorship language strongly implies no sponsorship; work auth classified as no-sponsorship
No years-of-experience floor stated, which may obscure true seniority expectations for candidates
High buzzword density ('agentic', 'AI agents', 'first-principles') makes scope harder to assess precisely
Founding role with significant ambiguity — no existing team, roadmap, or GTM motion in place
Role requires both deep technical credibility AND commercial/GTM ownership simultaneously — a wide bar

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, 'senior role' language, and founding scope)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed directly in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from customer descriptions and product context)
location.work_auth (inferred from E-Verify participation language and absence of sponsorship statement)
location.visa_sponsorship (inferred as 'no' from E-Verify statement and no explicit sponsorship offer)
ambiguities: Visa sponsorship is not explicitly addressed. E-Verify participation is disclosed but this does not confirm or deny sponsorship. Classified as 'no' and 'no-sponsorship' as a conservative reading; candidates should confirm with recruiter.

The 'ideally at a B2B SaaS company' qualifier on the zero-to-one requirement makes it borderline must-have vs. nice-to-have. The domain caveat ('Domain matters less') suggests it is a soft preference, so it is captured in nice_to_haves as well as contextually noted in the primary must_have requirement.
JD says 'This is a senior role' but explicitly deprioritizes years of experience in favor of impact — level signals are mixed.
